issues.summary: Okta App creation, Testing and Cutover sonarqube-stg(eks-use1-ep-test-1) service
issues.description:
### **Context**

As SonarQube STG  is installed on eks-use1-ep-test-1. A new okta app has to be created as sonarqube is saml authentication with okta, SAML apps only support a single ACS URL (sso_url, recipient, destination are all scalar strings in okta_app_saml).  
As Do a few last steps like testing it and doing curover to new Account

---

### Requirements

* We MUST Create a new Okta app `sonarqube-stg-eks` 

    * Must Verify OKTA sso, should redirect to okta
    
* We must do a few Tests

    * Verify DNS resolves 
    * Verify UI loads 
    
* We MUST test it(Scan results, Pipeline trigger etc)
* We MUST update any finding in Doc : <custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-0">https://adgear.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/ENG/pages/22859546850/SonarQube+Migration+plan+to+EKS+cluster</custom>

issues.summary: [POC] Enable EKS to JFrog connectivity via AWS PrivateLink
issues.description:
## Purpose

Routes all Artifactory traffic from EKS cluster `eks-use1-ep-dev-1` through AWS PrivateLink instead of NAT Gateways. This keeps traffic entirely within the AWS backbone, eliminates public internet exposure for artifact pulls, and significantly reduces data processing costs.

---

## Requirements

* All traffic to [adgear.jfrog.io](http://adgear.jfrog.io) MUST be going through private link
* We MUST make it work on `us-east-1`region on a single EKS cluster `eks-use1-ep-dev-1`
* We MUST research how to enable it potentially in a different region (we could reach out to jfrog support for help)
* We MUST present it to the team
* If it works we COULD consider enabling this on Rancher clusters as well, and create subsequent tickets

---

## Potential plan

* Add Interface VPC Endpoint for JFrog to `terraform-aws-global-network-subnets` module
* Register each `vpce-xxxx` endpoint ID in MyJFrog portal — **must be done by Marek or Dariusz or Nitish (JFrog admins)**
* Create Route53 Private Hosted Zone per VPC: `adgear.jfrog.io` → CNAME → `adgear.pe.jfrog.io`
* Validate in dev cluster (`eks-use1-ep-dev-1`) — confirm traffic resolves to private IP

From Jfrog support regarding cross regions vpc links:

_For environments located in a different region than_ [_adgear.jfrog.io_](http://adgear.jfrog.io) _(us-east-1), you will need to enable **cross-region support**. During Step 1: create the Endpoints in AWS, you will need to select the Enable cross Region endpoint checkbox (Step 5 of Create VPC endpoint) when configuring the endpoint in your AWS infrastructure._

‌

---

## References

* JFrog docs: [https://docs.jfrog.com/administration/docs/configure-cloud-security#step-1-create-the-endpoint-in-aws](https://docs.jfrog.com/administration/docs/configure-cloud-security#step-1-create-the-endpoint-in-aws)
* MyJFrog portal: [https://my.jfrog.com](https://my.jfrog.com)

issues.summary: Migrate SonarQube STG on use1-rdev-stg to eks-use1-ep-test-1
issues.description:
### **Context**

We run SonarQube STG on <custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-0">https://github.com/adgear/ep-sonarqube/blob/main/charts/use1-rdev-stg.yaml</custom> we would like to migrate it to eks-use1-ep-test-1.

---

### Requirements

* We MUST migrate SonarQube STG to eks-use1-ep-test-1
* We MUST prepare a migration procedure how to migrate SonarQube PRD with minimal downtime

    * We could bootstrap SonarQube STG on EKS next to SonarQube STG on Rancher, two will run parallel
    * The clients can use old one, after SonarQube STG on EKS we would shift the traffic
    * If the migration takes up-to 4h, we don’t care if we loose 4h worth of data from SonarQube scans, let’s keep it simple
    
* We MUST reach out to SonarQube support and most probably ask them for a new license for SonarQube STG in new cluster, we should explain them that we are migrating it from one cluster to the other
* At the end we MUST remove SonarQube STG from <custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-1">https://github.com/adgear/ep-sonarqube/blob/main/charts/use1-rdev-stg.yaml</custom>

---

### Other

* Be careful SonarQube STG and PRD runs in the same cluster use1-rdev, just in a different namespaces
* SonarQube is using RDS, we need to migrate it somehow

issues.summary: Migrate Listmonk to EKS eks-use1-ep-prod-1 with domain rename
issues.description:
### **Context**

Listmonk is currently deployed on the Rancher cluster `use1-rdev` via the Helm chart in `github.com/adgear/ep-listmonk`. The service uses an RDS PostgreSQL database which data must be preserved.

This task covers deploying Listmonk to `eks-use1-ep-prod-1`, migrating all data, and switching the public-facing domain from `ep-listmonk.int.adgear.com` to `ep-listmonk.int.samsungads.com`.

### **Requirements**

* Listmonk MUST be running on `eks-use1-ep-prod-1` and managed via ArgoCD.
* The public domain MUST be `ep-listmonk.int.samsungads.com`.
* It MUST be privately accessible, from the office.
* All existing RDS data (subscribers, campaigns, templates, configuration) MUST be intact after migration.

    * RDS subnet MUST be available in `eks-use1-ep-prod-1`if not create it.
    
* We WILL migrate IAM users on a new ticket (together with migration of SES).
* We WILL migrate SES configuration on new ticket.
* We MUST do a PoC using SES intead of SMTP to sending e-mails.
* EP Data MUST be notified.
* Listmonk MUST be removed from `use1-rdev`.
* Old DNS entries (`ep-listmonk.int.adgear.com`, `ep-listmonk.use1-rdev.k8s.adgear.com`) and old infra, MUST be cleaned up.

### **Assumptions**

* Downtime is acceptable; no zero-downtime cutover required.
* Only EP Data needs to be notified before cutover (EP Core is aware).

_**As a**_ EP Core team member working on Grafana  
_**I want to have**_ latest possible Grafana version  
_**So that I can**_ use new features and fix security problems

---

### Context

We did an upgrade to 11.6 version of Grafana. We have to finalize grafana upgrade story and migrate to 12.x.

---

### Requirements

* We MUST review breaking changes
* We MUST follow docs how to fix problems with breaking changes
* We SHOULD inform tenants if there are any breaking changes
* We MUST upgrade to latest possible version
* We HAVE to verify if all plugins / dashboards functions properly after upgrade

issues.summary: Grant EP Data team a dedicated ArgoCD project with admin access
issues.description:
## Context

We have decided in [this slack thread](https://github.com/adgear/k8s-core) that EP Data should start using EP Core ArgoCD, and be able to deploy their applications using <custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-0">https://github.com/adgear/k8s-core</custom> till now they used <custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-1">https://github.com/adgear/ep-tenant-operators/</custom> .

But we need to put some safeguards on it. We should separate EP Core apps from EP Data ones.

EP Data team  now has limited, hardcoded per-app read/sync access via `role:cg-ep-data-readsync`:

```
g, A.ArgoCD.cg-ep-data.Admin, role:cg-ep-data-readsync
p, role:cg-ep-data-readsync, applications, get, k8s-core/kafka-topics, allow
p, role:cg-ep-data-readsync, applications, sync, k8s-core/kafka-topics, allow
...
```

This approach does not scale — every new app they own requires new RBAC lines maintained by EP Core. The EP Data team should be self-sufficient for their own applications.

## Requirements

* We MUST create a separate ArgoCD project `cg-ep-data` for EP Data
* We MUST give EP Data team **full admin** access to their project `cg-ep-data`
* We MUST give them read only access to EP Core applications
* We MUST move all existing EP Data apps (like kafka-topics, maybe more) to project `cg-ep-data`, but only if this is **NON-DESTRUCTIVE**

    * We MUST not touch tenant operators
    

## Implementation Plan

Below is a suggestion

Created using Claude, verify the plan on your own.

### 1. In <custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-2">https://github.com/adgear/k8s-core</custom> create `templates/project.cg-ep-data.yaml`

```yaml
apiVersion: argoproj.io/v1alpha1
kind: AppProject
metadata:
  name: cg-ep-data
spec:
  clusterResourceWhitelist:
  - group: '*'
    kind: '*'
  destinations:
  {{- range $ns, $enabled := .Values.cgEpDataDestinationNamespaces }}
  {{- if $enabled }}
  - namespace: {{$ns}}
    server: https://kubernetes.default.svc
  {{- end }}
  {{- end }}
  sourceRepos:

issues.summary: Investigate: Alerting likely not working on EKS clusters
issues.description:
## Context

ArgoCD alerting appears to be silently broken on EKS clusters. The issue was discovered when `velero` on `eks-use1-ep-dev-1` went **OutOfSync + Missing** (health) and no alert was received.

Don’t fix velero, first fix alerting to receive alert about velero being out of sync.

## Concrete example

`velero` on `eks-use1-ep-dev-1` has been OutOfSync since at least 2026-05-27. Visible in ArgoCD UI at: [https://argocd.eks-use1-ep-dev-1.k8s-dev.samsungads.com](https://argocd.eks-use1-ep-dev-1.k8s-dev.samsungads.com).

The metric `argocd_app_info{name="velero", sync_status!="Synced"}` is present in VictoriaMetrics. The alert rule `ArgoAppNotSynced` (12h `for` duration) should have fired — but no alert appeared in Karma and no notification was sent.

## Requirements

* We MUST make sure alerting in EKS works (it delivers to Karma and PagerDuty).

## Possible Root cause analysis

### Alertmanager routes everything to `void`

Even if `vmalert-main` were to fire an alert, the alertmanager config in `ep-monitoring-eks/monitoring/values.yaml` has no real receivers configured:

```yaml
vmalertmanagers:
  main:
    spec:
      configRawYaml: |
        route:
          receiver: "void"
        receivers:
          - name: "void"
```

The cluster-specific override (`monitoring/eks-use1-ep-dev-1.yaml`) adds no Slack/PagerDuty routes.

## Affected clusters

All EKS clusters.

issues.summary: Enforce ECR Security Compliance via SCP – Mandatory Security Tag, Scan on Push & KMS Encryption
issues.description:
_**As a**_ Cloud Security & EP Core team member managing AWS ECR repositories,  
_**I want to have**_ security controls enforced at the organizational level for all new ECR repositories,  
_**So that I can**_ prevent new security findings for ECRs and ensure compliance with ISC Controls (0.3.B, 3.5.B, and 3.5.C)

---

### Context

During ISC Inspection following action items were decided to improve security compliance for AWS ECR repos and prevent future security findings:

1. **Enforce security review as mandatory for new ECRs** – Mandate a required tag with key `samsungads.com/securityTicket` and value `SECO-xxxx` on every new ECR repository.
2. **Enforce ECR image vulnerability scan on push** – Ensure all ECR repositories have "Scan on push" enabled.
3. **Enforce ECR image KMS encryption** – Ensure all ECR repositories use KMS encryption at rest (not the default S3-managed encryption).

These enforcements will prevent new findings for the following 3 Samsung AWS/ISC Controls:

* _Control 0.3.B – Have the container repositories in use been approved?_ → Requiring the `samsungads.com/securityTicket` tag ensures every new ECR has an associated security review/approval (SECO-xxxx).
* _Control 3.5.B – Was a vulnerability scan/measures completed for the ECR image?_ → Enabling "Scan on push" ensures vulnerability scanning is activated for every ECR image.
* _Control 3.5.C – Have the ECR images been encrypted?_ → Enforcing KMS encryption ensures ECR images are encrypted with KMS (not just the default Amazon S3 managed key).

The proposed implementation mechanism is **SCP (Service Control Policy)**, but alternative approaches SHOULD be evaluated if they provide a better solution.

---

### Requirements

* ===== THIS ISSUE IS NOT GROOMED =====
* An SCP MUST be implemented to enforce the following for all new ECR repositories:

    * A required tag `samsungads.com/securityTicket` with value matching pattern `SECO-xxxx` – creation of ECR without this tag MUST be denied.
    *

### **Context**

Create prod EKS cluster for EP **eks-euw1-se-tooling-dev-1** in AWS account: `samsungads-se-tooling-dev`.

---

### Requirements

* We MUST create new EKS cluster **eks-euw1-se-tooling-dev-1**
* It MUST be connected to Global Network to DEV segment.
* **Discuss network size on EKS weekly sync or daily**

    * The expected workload is “small”.
    * Don’t create subnets we don’t need like, rds, emr, etc.
    
* We MUST allow to connect to EKS cluster using permission sets for EP Core.
* It MUST be connected to Vault PRD.
* It MUST be provisioned in new `samsungads-se-tooling-dev` AWS account.
* After it is done we need to announce it to <custom data-type="mention" data-id="id-0">@Colin Borins</custom> .
* They should use Multi tenant ArgoCD.

---

### Assumptions

* Nothing is running on this cluster

## Context

The <custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-0">https://github.com/adgear/terraform/tree/master/aws/adgearprod/nacl-block</custom> Terraform configuration has accumulated several issues that make it unreliable, non-idempotent, and hard to maintain. This ticket tracks the cleanup work needed to bring it to a consistent and trustworthy state.

## Problems

### 1. Stale IAM role references

Some sub-stacks (e.g. `ssp-test/variables.tf`) still reference IAM roles that no longer exist. Provider configuration should be unified to use named AWS profiles, as already done in `adgearprod/provider.tf`.

### 2. Missing `.terraform.lock.hcl`

The provider lock file is absent. Without it, provider versions are non-deterministic across runs and contributors.

### 3. Unapplied stacks

Several sub-stacks have pending changes that have never been applied, leaving the real infrastructure out of sync with the Terraform state.

### 4. Dead VPC references

Some stacks reference VPCs that no longer exist in AWS. These need to be identified and removed to prevent apply errors and confusion.

### 5. `count` instead of `for_each` — non-idempotent changes

Resources are managed with `count` rather than `for_each`. This means any reordering or removal causes index shifts, leading to unintended resource replacements instead of targeted changes.

## Requirements

* We MUST fix all issues 1-5 on all stack under: <custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-1">https://github.com/adgear/terraform/tree/master/aws/adgearprod/nacl-block</custom> which belong to EP.

issues.summary: Implement JFrog Kubelet Credential Provider on EKS (passwordless image pulls) [EKS]
issues.description:
## Goal

Implement the [JFrog Kubelet Credential Provider](https://github.com/jfrog/jfrog-credentials-provider) on EKS clusters to eliminate long lived static credentials. The provider runs as a DaemonSet and intercepts image pull requests, dynamically exchanging AWS node identity for short-lived Artifactory tokens.

## Benefits

* Security, automatic token rotation, if token leaked, scope would be limited
* We would have artifactory service user per cluster, we could tell who is using artifactory better

## **Reference documentation**

* https://github.com/jfrog/jfrog-credentials-provider
* [https://github.com/jfrog/jfrog-credentials-provider/blob/main/AWS.md](https://github.com/jfrog/jfrog-credentials-provider/blob/main/AWS.md) 

## Explanation how the mechanism works

 It plugs into a native Kubernetes feature: kubelet credential providers.

```
  Pod needs image from adgear.jfrog.io
           │
           ▼
     Kubelet matches image pattern → executes binary on the node
           │
           ▼
     Binary calls EC2 metadata service → gets node's IAM role credentials
           │
           ▼
     Binary signs a STS GetCallerIdentity request using those credentials
     (this is a signed HTTP request proving "I am role X" — no actual STS call made yet)
           │
           ▼
     Binary sends the signed request to Artifactory
     POST /access/api/v1/aws/token
           │
           ▼
     Artifactory validates the signature + looks up IAM role ARN in its bindings
     → finds the mapped Artifactory user
     → generates a short-lived registry token for that user
           │
           ▼
     Binary returns { username, token } to kubelet (via stdout, JSON protocol)
           │
           ▼
     Kubelet uses token to pull image from Artifactory
           │
           ▼
     Token cached in kubelet process memory for defaultCacheDuration (e.g. 5h)
     No imagePullSecrets in the pod spec needed
```

## Additional information

There is already mechanism w

### **Context**

We must migrate Golinks on <custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-0">https://github.com/adgear/golinks-helm-chart/blob/master/use1-rdev.yaml</custom> to eks-use1-ep-prod-1.

---

### Requirements

* We MUST migrate it to eks-use1-ep-prod-1 cluster.

    * We should do it ONLINE. We should run golinks on eks-use1-ep-prod-1  parallel to use1-rdev, and we should switch the traffic, DNS to it.
    
* We MUST use ArgoCD.
* We MUST make sure alerting works, we are paged.
* We MUST remove Golinks use1-rdev instance from Rancher at the end.
* We MUST inform tenants.

* The same person that did <custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-1">https://adgear.atlassian.net/browse/PEEPC-2263</custom>  MUST do this ticket.
* We should provision it under two domains:

    * [http://golinks.int.adgear.com](http://golinks.int.adgear.com/)
    * [http://golinks.int.samsungads.com/](http://golinks.int.adgear.com/)
    
* We must announce that [http://golinks.int.adgear.com](http://golinks.int.adgear.com) will be deprecated after one month.

---

### Other

Be aware that there are some unfixed issues with restoration mechanism: <custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-2">https://adgear.atlassian.net/browse/PEEPC-885</custom> , <custom data-type="mention" data-id="id-3">@Marek Barczyk</custom> knows all the details

issues.summary: Do a Poc testing migration path of ingress-NGINX using Traefik
issues.description:
**As a** EP owner,  
**I want** to find an alternative for NGINX controller in Rancher/EKS,  
**so that I can** avoid breaking existing applications while maintaining a secure posture in production.

---

### **Context**

As an outcome of the task <custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-0">https://adgear.atlassian.net/browse/PEEPC-1968</custom> , EP decided to do a proof of concept with Traefik to have a similar scenario as we have today with ingress-NGINX (which is announced to be deprecated at the end of march 2026).  
  
More information:  
  
<custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-1">https://doc.traefik.io/traefik/migrate/nginx-to-traefik/</custom> 

[Traefik Kubernetes Ingress NGINX Routing Configuration - Traefik](https://doc.traefik.io/traefik/reference/routing-configuration/kubernetes/ingress-nginx/)

---

### Requirements

* We MUST check the traefik ingress controller to substitute the current ingress-NGINX

    * The alternative MUST require minimal migration effort
    * We MUST evaluate all annotations used in Ingress (are compatible with the new solution)
    
* Engineering teams MUST be able to expose internal services easily
* Engineering teams SHOULD not require any approval or PR creation, it should be self-served
* We MUST continue the POC in eks-use1-test-2 (EKS) or use1-ep-test-2 (Rancher)
* We MUST present the outcome to the team
* We MUST document it
